label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage to find out which statement is true. The question asks which of the four options (A to D) is true based on the passage. Let's analyze each option: (A) Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record. The passage says: "But only about 90,000 adult salmon returned to the Central Valley last fall--the second lowest number on record..." This matches exactly with option A. So option A appears to be true. (B) The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up. The passage says: "wild salmon are born in streams and rivers, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles..." This indicates salmon migrate to the ocean when they're young (juveniles), not when they "grow up" (which would imply they're adults). So option B is incorrect. (C) The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutrients as the wild salmon. The passage mentions there will be "abundant supplies of farm-raised salmon, but prices could be higher." However, it doesn't compare the taste or nutritional value of farm-raised vs. wild salmon. So option C is not supported by the passage. (D) Salmon fishing off the coasts of California and Oregon is strictly forbidden. The passage says the council will choose between three management options: a total ban on salmon fishing, limited fishing in select areas, or catch-and-release fishing
